name: New Zealand
summary: Strong fit for a Bangladeshi software-engineer couple: Software Engineer is Green List Tier 1 (straight-to-residence), master's grads get up to 3y open post-study work, partner of a level 9 student works freely, citizenship in 5y with dual allowed. Main frictions: no NZ mission in Dhaka with elevated student-visa refusals, high CS tuition, smaller/lower-paying tech market than AU/US.

job-market.summary: Software Engineer on Green List Tier 1; AI/data/cyber demand rising, but small isolated market with lower pay than AU/US.
job-market.reasoning: Both spouses are software engineers, an occupation INZ classes as a skill shortage on the Green List Tier 1, and 2025 data show data-science roles up ~24% and cybersecurity up ~27% with new dedicated AI roles. Demand is real and sponsorship is accessible, but New Zealand is a geographically isolated, small market with fewer large employers and mid-tier rate compression, so it scores below an AU/US-scale market.
evidence: Software Engineer and ICT Security Specialist sit on Green List Tier 1 (Straight to Residence).
2025: data-science job postings up ~24%, cybersecurity up ~27%; AI/data-engineering roles emerging.
Median wage NZD 35.00/hr (from 9 Mar 2026); SE salaries ~NZD 90k-160k, below AU/US levels.
Remote-first hiring compressed mid-tier rates; demand skews to fewer senior/architecture/AI roles.

visa-access.summary: No NZ mission in Dhaka; fully online via INZ, but Bangladeshi student-visa success only ~45% plus Genuine Student bar.
visa-access.reasoning: There is no New Zealand mission in Dhaka; Bangladeshi nationals apply online directly to Immigration New Zealand and receive decisions electronically, so no travel is required. However, the reported 2025-26 student-visa success rate for Bangladeshis is around 45%, with a compulsory Genuine Student statement and funds held 5-6 months beforehand, marking a meaningful financial and credibility bar.
evidence: Applications lodged online with INZ; outcomes issued electronically (no Dhaka visa office).
Reported Bangladeshi student-visa success rate ~45% in 2025-26 (varies by institution/application quality).
Genuine Student statement now compulsory; police clearance and INZ panel-physician medical required.
Funds must be held 5-6 months pre-application to prove maintenance.

citizenship.summary: Citizenship after ~5y residence (1,350 days), dual citizenship allowed, English assessed informally - favourable.
citizenship.reasoning: Citizenship by grant requires holding residence and being present at least 1,350 days over five years (min 240 days/year). New Zealand permits dual citizenship and Bangladesh allows it too, so the couple keep their Bangladeshi passports. English is assessed informally during the application rather than by a fixed test, which suits fluent IT professionals; a formal test is only planned from mid-2027.
evidence: Citizenship by grant: ~5 years residence, 1,350 days total presence (240 days/year minimum).
Dual citizenship explicitly allowed by New Zealand.
English requirement assessed informally by DIA through the application process.
Formal citizenship test only proposed from mid-2027.

post-study-work.summary: Master's grads get up to 3y open Post Study Work Visa; unrestricted employer/role, easy switch to skilled work.
post-study-work.reasoning: A master's degree completed with at least 30 weeks of full-time study qualifies for a Post Study Work Visa of up to 3 years with open conditions, letting both spouses work for any employer and switch into an Accredited Employer Work Visa or Green List residence. A 2024 PGDip-to-master's bridge further protects eligibility.
evidence: Master's grads eligible for up to 3-year open PSWV (min 30 weeks full-time study).
Nov 2024: PGDip-to-master's progression preserves PSWV eligibility; 12-month application window.
Open work conditions allow free job change and transition to AEWV / Green List residence.
Both engineers can each hold their own PSWV.

spouse-family.summary: Partner of a level 9 (master's) student gets a work visa regardless of field; both engineers can each hold own skilled visa.
spouse-family.reasoning: Critically, both spouses are software engineers, so neither depends on the other: each can hold their own student, PSW or Green List visa. Even in the study phase, the partner of a student on a level 9 (master's) programme can obtain a work visa irrespective of subject, and from Dec 2024 partners of AEWV holders in skilled roles get open work rights, giving full spouse work entitlement throughout.
evidence: Partner of a level 9/10 (master's/PhD) student can get a work visa regardless of study field.
From 2 Dec 2024, partners of AEWV holders in skilled roles get open work rights (wage thresholds apply).
Both spouses are engineers - each can independently hold student/PSW/Green List status.
Dependent children can accompany on visitor/student visas.

links.title: Partner of a Student Work Visa (INZ)

msc-study.summary: English-native MSc widely available, but high CS tuition (NZD ~35k-54k/yr); 25h/wk work, partner accompanies.
msc-study.reasoning: New Zealand universities teach master's degrees in English with no language transition, and students may work up to 25 hours/week from Nov 2025 while the partner accompanies and works. The drawback is cost: international MS Computer Science tuition runs roughly NZD 35,000-54,000/year plus NZD 1,200-2,500/month living, and broad scholarships for self-funded Bangladeshi applicants are limited.
evidence: MS Computer Science tuition ~NZD 35,000-54,000/year for international students.
Living costs ~NZD 1,200-2,500/month; insurance/visa fees add ~NZD 3,500 upfront.
From 3 Nov 2025 eligible students may work up to 25 hours/week in semester.
Partner of master's student can accompany and work; broad funded scholarships scarce for self-funded BD applicants.

pr-pathway.summary: Green List Tier 1 Software Engineer = Straight to Residence; apply for residence immediately on qualifying job offer.
pr-pathway.reasoning: Software Engineer is a Green List Tier 1 role, which is a Straight to Residence pathway: with a qualifying Accredited Employer job offer meeting the wage requirement, the couple can apply for a resident visa immediately, with no minimum NZ work period. The alternative 6-point Skilled Migrant Category remains available. Some specialist IT roles carry a higher wage threshold, which slightly tempers certainty.
evidence: Software Engineer = Green List Tier 1 (Straight to Residence) - no set work period before applying.
Tier 2 'Work to Residence' roles need 24 months work; Tier 1 does not.
SMC 6-point system available as alternative; Aug 2026 changes simplify wage thresholds.
Some specialist IT roles require pay above median wage to qualify.

income-cost.summary: Decent SE pay (~NZD 90k-160k) but high Auckland cost and tech salaries below AU/US trim net savings.
income-cost.reasoning: Software engineer salaries of roughly NZD 90,000-160,000 are solid against a cost of living about 10-15% below Australia, supporting a dual-income couple. However, Auckland rent is high, NZ tech pay sits clearly below Australian and US levels, and purchasing power favours Australia, so net savings are moderate rather than strong.
evidence: SE salaries ~NZD 90,000-160,000; junior ~NZD 65,000-85,000.
Auckland frugal living ~NZD 2,400-2,800/month; rent charged weekly, NZD 180-280/wk per room.
NZ ~10-15% cheaper than Australia, but AU/US salaries higher; purchasing power favours AU.
Dual software income materially improves household savings.

healthcare.summary: Universal publicly funded healthcare for residents/work-visa holders; students need insurance during study.
healthcare.reasoning: Once on a resident or qualifying 2+ year work visa, the couple access New Zealand's publicly funded universal healthcare with heavily subsidised care, a clear quality and cost advantage. During the study phase they must hold private student health insurance, a minor cost, which keeps the score just below top tier.
evidence: Publicly funded universal healthcare for residents and most 2+ year work-visa holders.
International students must hold approved medical/travel insurance (~NZD 900/yr).
Subsidised GP visits and prescriptions for eligible residents.
High-quality public hospital system.

links.title: Eligibility for publicly funded health services

culture-language.summary: English-native country; no language barrier for fluent IT professionals - very favourable.
culture-language.reasoning: New Zealand is English-speaking across study, work and daily life, so two English-fluent software engineers face essentially no language barrier. The multicultural, immigrant-heavy population (especially in Auckland) further eases settlement.
evidence: English is the primary working and academic language.
No language test required for citizenship beyond informal conversational ability.
Highly multicultural Auckland with large migrant communities.
IELTS/PTE used only for study/visa entry, not for daily integration.

safety-law.summary: Top-tier rule of law (WJP #6 high-income, 2024), low crime, strong rights protections.
safety-law.reasoning: New Zealand ranks 6th of 47 high-income countries in the 2024 WJP Rule of Law Index and is among the few countries whose score rose, with fundamental-rights protections holding up where they fell elsewhere. Low crime and stable institutions make it a very safe destination for the couple.
evidence: WJP Rule of Law Index 2024: 6th of 47 high-income countries; overall score rose 1.0%.
Fundamental Rights factor held up while it fell in 81% of countries 2016-2024.
Top performer in East Asia & Pacific alongside Australia and Japan.
Generally low violent-crime rates.

links.title: WJP Rule of Law Index - New Zealand

politics.summary: Stable democracy; 2025 reforms actively court skilled migrants (NOL, AEWV easing, Green List residence).
politics.reasoning: New Zealand is a stable parliamentary democracy, and 2025 policy is actively migrant-friendly: a National Occupation List added 91 occupations, the AEWV median-wage threshold was removed and required experience cut to two years, investor rules eased, and student work hours raised. This pro-skilled-migration stance directly benefits the couple.
evidence: From 3 Nov 2025, National Occupation List replaced ANZSCO with 91 occupations added.
AEWV median-wage threshold removed; required experience cut 3 to 2 years.
Investor visa simplified (Apr 2025); student work hours raised to 25/week.
Stable institutions; orderly democratic transitions.

tax.summary: No general CGT, no separate social-security tax; top rate 39%, GST 15%, modest 1.67% ACC levy.
tax.reasoning: New Zealand has no general capital gains tax or wealth tax and no separate social-security payroll tax, only a modest ~1.67% ACC earner's levy, which keeps the effective burden on two software salaries relatively low. The top marginal rate of 39% applies only above NZD 180,000, with GST at 15%.
evidence: No general capital gains tax and no wealth tax.
No dedicated social-security tax; ACC earner's levy ~1.67%.
GST 15%; KiwiSaver contributions optional.

muslim-diaspora.summary: ~60k Muslims (Auckland ~40k), mosques and halal present but relatively small community; strong post-2019 solidarity.
muslim-diaspora.reasoning: New Zealand's Muslim population is around 60,000 (about 40,000 in Auckland) across 40+ ethnicities including South Asians, with 30+ mosques and Islamic agencies in Auckland and halal food available, so practising is feasible. It remains a relatively small community, and while the 2019 Christchurch attack is part of the context, it was followed by strong national solidarity and protective measures.
evidence: Muslim population ~60,000 nationally; ~40,000 in Auckland.
30+ mosques and Muslim agencies in Auckland; Deans Ave mosque rebuilt in Christchurch.
Community spans 40+ ethnicities incl. South Asians; halal food accessible in main cities.
Post-2019 Christchurch: strong solidarity, protective legislation and community support.
